name: Nigerian Pidgin (Naijá)
notes: Nigerian Pidgin / Naijá, an English-lexifier creole of Nigeria. STUB: the grapheme inventory is NOT yet specified. Script: Latin alphabet. There is no single accepted orthography: writing ranges from ad-hoc English-based spelling to the phonemic Naijá orthography proposed by the Naijá Langwej Akedemi, and the two give completely different letter-to-phoneme mappings for the same word, so no honest single table can be given. A full spec must also model the substrate-influenced vowel system (7 vowels) and tone. Wikidata gives no ISO 639-3 or Glottolog code on this item (it is the Nigerian Pidgin Wikipedia), so those fields are left null rather than guessed; the language's own ISO code is pcm. Input contract: a specific orthography (English-based vs Naijá phonemic) must be chosen before a grapheme table can apply.
